tossups.question: This author recalled a choice of “Porridge or Prunes” for breakfast—which epitomize the grey “spirit of gastronomic joylessness”—in an essay for Food and Wine magazine that inspired Julian Barnes to re-evaluate this author’s work. An essay by this author posits that civilization arises in the gaps between eras of force, and claims that cultivating “personal relationships” is the key to living in the “Age of Faith” on the eve of the Second World War. A book by this author includes a pageant play narrated by a Woodman. This author’s essay (*) “What I Believe” is the source of the maxim that one should sooner rather betray one’s country than one’s friend. This author of the essay collections Abinger (“ABB-in-jer”) Harvest and Two Cheers for Democracy wrote a novel in which a man dies after he is beaten with a sword, causing a bookcase to fall onto him. For 10 points, who created the Wilcoxes and the Schlegels in Howards End?
tossups.question_sanitized: This author recalled a choice of "Porridge or Prunes" for breakfast-which epitomize the grey "spirit of gastronomic joylessness"-in an essay for Food and Wine magazine that inspired Julian Barnes to re-evaluate this author's work. A book by this author includes a pageant play narrated by a Woodman. This author's essay (*) "What I Believe" is the source of the maxim that one should sooner rather betray one's country than one's friend. This author of the essay collections Abinger ("ABB-in-jer") Harvest and Two Cheers for Democracy wrote a novel in which a man dies after he is beaten with a sword, causing a bookcase to fall onto him. For 10 points, who created the Wilcoxes and the Schlegels in Howards End?

tossups.question: During a chase in one of this author’s novels, the pursuer finds a man’s hiding spot behind a stove, grabs him by his broken arm, and begins to strangle him. After jumping out of a moving vehicle and cutting her hand, a woman in one of this author’s novels thinks, “the whole system’s wrong, and [I] must challenge it” when she learns a chauffeur tackled a girl whose cat was hit by a car. A woman in one of this author’s novels screams, “I stole it! It slipped from my arms!” after losing her grip on a baby during a (*) carriage accident in which it dies. At the end of that novel by this author, Caroline Abbot tells Philip Herriton that she loves the cruel Gino Carrella. After her husband Henry Wilcox “refused to connect,” the protagonist of one of this author’s novels goes to stay with her pregnant sister Helen Schlegel at the title estate. For 10 points, name this author of Where Angels Fear to Tread and Howards End.
tossups.question_sanitized: During a chase in one of this author's novels, the pursuer finds a man's hiding spot behind a stove, grabs him by his broken arm, and begins to strangle him. After jumping out of a moving vehicle and cutting her hand, a woman in one of this author's novels thinks, "the whole system's wrong, and [I] must challenge it" when she learns a chauffeur tackled a girl whose cat was hit by a car. A woman in one of this author's novels screams, "I stole it! It slipped from my arms!" after losing her grip on a baby during a (*) carriage accident in which it dies. At the end of that novel by this author, Caroline Abbot tells Philip Herriton that she loves the cruel Gino Carrella. After her husband Henry Wilcox "refused to connect," the protagonist of one of this author's novels goes to stay with her pregnant sister Helen Schlegel at the title estate. For 10 points, name this author of Where Angels Fear to Tread and Howards End.

tossups.question: The waiter Gennaro dies after trying to release the boy Eustace into the wild after Eustace was visited by the god Pan in this man's short story "The Story of a Panic." A science fiction story by this author, features Kuno telling his mother the title phrase before civilization ends. The protagonist of another of his stories returns to his deceased brother after being offered the choice to go through two gates following his crossing of the title boundary. Along with "The Machine Stops" and (*) "The Other Side of the Hedge," a story by this author features Dante saying "I am the food and not the life" to Mr. Bons, who dies after stepping out of the title vehicle, "The Celestial Omnibus." In a novel by this man, Lucy Honeychurch and George witness a murder before eventually getting married. For 10 points, name this author of A Room With a View.
tossups.question_sanitized: The waiter Gennaro dies after trying to release the boy Eustace into the wild after Eustace was visited by the god Pan in this man's short story "The Story of a Panic." A science fiction story by this author, features Kuno telling his mother the title phrase before civilization ends. The protagonist of another of his stories returns to his deceased brother after being offered the choice to go through two gates following his crossing of the title boundary. Along with "The Machine Stops" and (*) "The Other Side of the Hedge," a story by this author features Dante saying "I am the food and not the life" to Mr. Bons, who dies after stepping out of the title vehicle, "The Celestial Omnibus." In a novel by this man, Lucy Honeychurch and George witness a murder before eventually getting married. For 10 points, name this author of A Room With a View.

bonuses.leadin: One character in this work gets hit in the chest by a bouquet while watching the mad scene in Lucia di Lammermoor. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel, in which Lilia Herriton’s marriage to an Italian dentist’s son results in her brother-in-law Phillip coming to Monteriano to convince her to marry someone of her class.

A similar scene to the Lammermoor scene occurs in Howard’s End when Helen, Margaret, Tibby and Fraulein Mosebach all interpret this musical work differently. Helen sees this work as a story involving heroes, goblins and shipwrecks, while Fraulein Mosebach just remembers that the composer is “echt Deutsch.”
Where Angels Fear to Tread and Howard’s End were written by this Edwardian author of A Room with a View.
answers: Ludwig van Beethoven’s fifth symphony in C minor [or obvious equivalents, such as Beethoven’s Symphony No. 5]
answers_sanitized: Ludwig van Beethoven's fifth symphony in C minor [or obvious equivalents, such as Beethoven's Symphony No. 5]

A similar scene to the Lammermoor scene occurs in Howard's End when Helen, Margaret, Tibby and Fraulein Mosebach all interpret this musical work differently. Helen sees this work as a story involving heroes, goblins and shipwrecks, while Fraulein Mosebach just remembers that the composer is "echt Deutsch."
Where Angels Fear to Tread and Howard's End were written by this Edwardian author of A Room with a View.

bonuses.leadin: The opening to this novel is an email from Jerome to his father Harold, a professor of Rembrandt studies. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel about the intertwined relationships between the Anglo-African-American Belsey family and the Trinidadian Kipps family. It takes its title from an Elaine Scarry book pairing the title quality with “being just.”

On Beauty was the second hit for this young British novelist, who wrote the forgettable book The Autograph Man. Groups with the acronyms FATE and KEVIN oppose the evil FutureMouse in her best-known novel, White Teeth.
The opening emails in On Beauty mirror Helen’s letters to her sister at the beginning of Howards End, penned by this British writer of A Room with a View.
